notes:
CERT/CC is the original computer security incident response team, established at Carnegie Mellon University's Software Engineering Institute (SEI) after the Morris Worm in 1988. CERT/CC coordinates vulnerability disclosure and publishes vulnerability notes. Funded by CISA through the SEI FFRDC.

"CERT" is a registered trademark — other "CERTs" worldwide are unrelated organizations. CERT/CC focuses on coordination; they don't scan or discover most vulnerabilities themselves. CERT/CC pioneered coordinated vulnerability disclosure and specializes in vulnerabilities affecting multiple vendors.

data.description: Detailed vulnerability analysis published by CERT/CC with coordination history, vendor responses, and multi-vendor impact assessment.
data.notes:
VU# Vulnerability Notes provide different value than CVE: coordination details (who was notified, timeline, vendor responses), multi-vendor focus (particularly valuable for protocol/library vulnerabilities), and deeper technical analysis than typical CVE descriptions. Most VU# entries have corresponding CVE IDs, but the VU# note contains additional coordination context.

VU# numbers predate CVE for some historical vulnerabilities. The VU# prefix includes a literal '#' character — the identifier format is VU#NNNNNN (e.g., VU#867593). When used in a SecID, the subpath after the SecID fragment separator '#' is the full identifier including its own '#': secid:advisory/cert.org/vu#VU#867593.

urls.note: Lookup URL uses only the numeric portion of the VU# identifier, not the VU# prefix.

children.description: CERT/CC Vulnerability Note ID (VU# followed by numeric ID)

number.description: Numeric portion of the VU# identifier (e.g., '867593' from 'VU#867593')
